description:
## Goal

Deliver the complete user-facing Settings experience after the backend App Settings interface and configurable Markdown renderer exist: app-shell settings state, safe automatic persistence, bottom-sidebar navigation, direct numeric entry, live preview, Reset, error recovery, and application to every Issue description/comment.

This is one integrated frontend slice because draft handling, latest-intent persistence, navigation lifetime, preview, and app-wide rendering are one user-visible transaction. Splitting them would either create unused state modules or temporary preview-only behavior.

## Dependencies and Current Territory

Requires:

- `bsm-al6.1`: generated typed App Settings load/update interface and dedicated persistence;
- `bsm-al6.2`: shared `MarkdownContent` base-size interface;
- transitively, `bsm-kia.5`: stable landed Workspace integration before `bsm-al6.1` extends the shared runtime/RPC surface.

The latest merged Workspace code is authoritative. This task must preserve its confirmed Workspace transition rules and must not reintroduce stale Workspace/Issue snapshots. `bsm-kia.6` is a possible later renderer-transition cleanup, not a prerequisite; if it lands first, integrate with its explicit model rather than duplicating it.

Current source facts to re-check at implementation time:

- `App` owns app-shell state and renders the stable sidebar plus main content.
- `IssueExplorer` owns Issue Search, selected Issue, and scrollable list/detail presentation locally; accidental unmounting currently loses that state.
- Workspace transitions may intentionally remount/reset Issue Explorer after a confirmed Current Workspace change.
- ADR-0004 requires generated TauRPC plus the established Effect integration style for new native calls.

## App-Shell Settings State

Create one focused frontend App Settings module/hook owned by the application shell, not by the Settings page. It stays alive while the user navigates between Settings and Issue Explorer.

Model these values explicitly:

1. **Draft**: the raw string currently in the number field, including empty or invalid partial input.
2. **Applied settings**: the newest valid user intent currently used by the preview, Issue descriptions, and comments.
3. **Confirmed settings**: the last value acknowledged as durably saved by the backend.
4. **Load state**: loading, loaded, or loaded-with-warning/default.
5. **Save state**: idle/saved, saving, or failed for a specific newest value.

Do not collapse draft, applied, and confirmed values into one variable. That would make invalid-entry and save-failure behavior ambiguous.

Use an Effect-backed frontend settings interface around the generated TauRPC proxy. React consumes typed results through one module rather than calling raw generated methods from controls. Keep the module specific to App Settings; do not build a generic repository-wide settings framework.

### Startup

- Start settings loading at app-shell startup independently from Workspace availability/loading.
- Missing persisted state resolves to applied/confirmed 14px without error.
- A load warning resolves to applied 14px and stores the warning for Settings presentation.
- Pass applied settings to Markdown rendering once resolved. If any Markdown can render before resolution, use the same 14px default so there is no invalid intermediate style; do not flash an arbitrary value.
- Settings navigation must remain available during loading and with no Current Workspace.

### Latest-intent automatic save

Every valid draft updates applied settings immediately and enters the save pipeline. The pipeline must serialize or coalesce writes so durable storage eventually matches the newest valid user intent.

Required ordering semantics:

- At most one backend update needs to be in flight; newer valid values may replace one pending queued value.
- If 18 is saving and the user enters 20 then 24, the system may skip persisting 20, but after settling it must persist 24.
- Completion of the 18 request must never mark 24 as saved.
- Failure of an older request must not replace a newer applied value or show a retry targeting the older value.
- After any completion, continue with the newest unsaved applied value.
- Navigating away from Settings does not cancel the queue.
- Do not debounce so long that closing immediately after a visible Saved state loses data. A short coalescing implementation is acceptable, but the status must represent actual backend confirmation.

### Save failure

- Keep the newest valid applied value active for the current desktop session.
- Keep the matching field draft intact.
- Show a persistent safe error explaining that the value is active but not saved and may revert after restart.
- Provide Retry for the newest valid applied value.
- A later valid edit implicitly becomes the new retry target and re-enters the queue.
- A successful save clears the save error and any prior load warning repaired by that deliberate write.

## App Destination and Sidebar UX

Introduce a small app-destination state, conceptually `issueExplorer | settings`; do not add a router.

- Add a semantic Settings button immediately above the Current Workspace selector at the bottom of the sidebar, separated from Issue List View controls.
- Use the existing icon system and visual language: compact, soft-square, tonal background/border, visible hover/focus, no pill treatment.
- The control remains enabled with no Current Workspace, during issue loading, and during Workspace memory recovery.
- When Settings is open, the Settings button is the sole `aria-current="page"` item.
- The remembered active Issue List View remains stored but is not simultaneously announced as the current page.
- Clicking any Issue List View while Settings is open returns to the Issue Explorer and selects that view. Returning to the already remembered view also works.
- Keep the Current Workspace selector visible and usable on Settings.

Opening Settings replaces the main pane, not the sidebar. Preserve same-Workspace Issue Explorer interaction state across the visit:

- active Issue List View;
- raw Issue Search query;
- selected Issue;
- Issue List scroll position;
- Issue Detail scroll position where the existing layout makes it independently scrollable.

Do not rely on accidental browser behavior. Either keep the Issue Explorer mounted but semantically hidden/inert while Settings is active, or lift the necessary state/scroll restoration to a stable owner. The implementation must not expose hidden Issue Explorer landmarks or controls to assistive technology.

If Current Workspace changes while Settings is open, preserve the Settings destination and app-wide typography, but let the existing confirmed Workspace commit reset workspace-scoped Issue Explorer interaction exactly as it does outside Settings. Pending/failed Workspace changes must not reset the old context.

## Settings Page Layout

Use a focused utility-page layout consistent with `docs/research/DESIGN.md`:

- semantic `main` landmark with an accessible Settings label;
- centered content with a compact maximum width around 400px and normal window scrolling;
- page heading `Settings`;
- section heading `Markdown Typography` plus concise explanation that the setting affects Issue descriptions and comments across all Workspaces;
- one standard 40px-height number input row with visible `px` suffix;
- Reset action near the field;
- validation/help text immediately associated with the field;
- subtle save status below the control;
- representative preview in a bordered tonal surface using the real Markdown renderer.

Leave visual room in the Markdown Typography section for later prose and monospace font-family fields, but do not render disabled placeholders or promise unavailable functionality.

## Number Input Contract

- Use a labeled native number input.
- Attributes: `min=8`, `max=72`, `step=1`; include appropriate desktop numeric input mode only if it improves the existing browser behavior.
- Initial draft mirrors the loaded/applied value.
- Valid values are canonical whole-number strings representing 8-72 inclusive. Leading/trailing whitespace may normalize; do not invent locale decimal parsing.
- Empty, partial, non-numeric, fractional, under-minimum, and over-maximum drafts are invalid.
- Invalid drafts set `aria-invalid`, reference an inline error through `aria-describedby`, do not alter applied Markdown, and do not enter the save queue.
- Correcting the draft clears validation and applies/saves the new value.
- Reset works from valid, invalid, saving, or failed states: it clears validation, sets draft/applied to `14`, and queues 14 for persistence.
- Reset may be disabled only when draft/applied/confirmed are all 14 and no error requires repair. It must remain available to repair an invalid draft or load warning.
- No slider, radio presets, separate code-size field, or explicit Save button.

## Live Preview Contract

Render one stable sample through `MarkdownContent` with the applied font size. It should be concise enough for a 400px utility page but cover both future font roles and the current scaling hierarchy:

- h2 or h3 heading;
- prose with emphasis and one safe link;
- unordered or ordered list;
- blockquote;
- inline code;
- short fenced code block;
- compact table if it fits without making the page noisy.

Label the preview region for assistive technology. It updates only when applied settings change, not for invalid drafts. It must use the same safe link opener/inert-link behavior as Issue content and no preview-only renderer/CSS.

## Error and Status Presentation

### Load warning

A malformed, unsupported, or unreadable stored document leaves the app usable at 14px. Settings shows a non-blocking persistent warning that the default is in use and that entering a valid value or Reset will attempt repair. Do not show a warning for genuinely missing first-run state.

### Save status

- `Saving`: visible while the newest applied value is not yet confirmed.
- `Saved`: visible only when confirmed equals newest applied value and no queued write remains.
- `Not saved`: visible with Retry after failure.

Use a polite live region only for meaningful settled transitions or errors; avoid announcing a rapid Saving/Saved pair on every arrow-key step. Field validation remains directly associated with the input.

## Application Integration

- Pass `appliedSettings.markdown.fontSizePx` from the app shell to every Issue description and comment `MarkdownContent` call.
- The preview receives the identical applied value and renderer interface.
- One value applies across all Workspaces and remains unchanged through Workspace switching/removal/reset.
- Do not apply it to Issue titles, section headings, metadata, comment author/date chrome, sidebar, search, input controls, or Workspace selector.
- Avoid prop drilling unrelated settings internals. Passing one typography value through the existing App -> IssueExplorer -> MarkdownContent composition is acceptable; a broad React Context is not required unless the merged tree demonstrates multiple independent branches that materially benefit.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Availability and navigation

- [ ] Settings appears immediately above the Current Workspace selector and is available with no Current Workspace or issue data.
- [ ] Opening Settings leaves the sidebar/Workspace selector visible and replaces only the main pane.
- [ ] Settings alone has current-page semantics while open.
- [ ] Selecting any Issue List View returns to Issue Explorer and applies that view.
- [ ] No modal, URL router, or generic navigation framework is introduced.

### State preservation

- [ ] Visiting Settings and returning in the same Workspace preserves active Issue List View, search draft, selected Issue, list scroll, and detail scroll.
- [ ] Hidden Issue Explorer content is not exposed to assistive technology while Settings is active.
- [ ] A confirmed Workspace change while Settings is active resets only workspace-scoped Issue Explorer state according to existing Workspace semantics.
- [ ] Pending/failed Workspace changes do not erase the old Issue Explorer context.

### Input, preview, and application

- [ ] One direct input supports whole-number 8-72px values and defaults to 14px; no slider/presets/save button exist.
- [ ] Every valid draft updates preview, descriptions, and comments immediately.
- [ ] Invalid/partial drafts show accessible inline validation and retain the last valid applied rendering.
- [ ] Reset clears invalid state, applies 14px, and enters the persistence pipeline.
- [ ] Preview content uses the shared safe Markdown renderer and represents prose, headings, lists, blockquote, inline code, fenced code, and compact structured content.
- [ ] Non-Markdown UI remains unchanged.

### Persistence lifecycle

- [ ] Startup loads App Settings independently of Workspace state.
- [ ] Missing settings use 14px silently; typed load warnings use 14px with repair guidance.
- [ ] Rapid valid changes cannot produce stale durable state or stale Saved status.
- [ ] Save failure keeps the newest value active, identifies it as not persisted, and retries that newest value.
- [ ] Navigation away does not cancel in-flight/queued saving.
- [ ] Successful edit/Reset after a load warning repairs state and clears the warning.

### Accessibility and design

- [ ] Semantic landmarks/headings, button names, field label, unit, range/help, validation, preview label, and error/status relationships are screen-reader discoverable.
- [ ] Every interaction is keyboard operable with visible focus.
- [ ] Page follows the compact Deep Carbon utility-screen language and avoids excessive pills/shadows.

## Frontend Test Matrix

Use React Testing Library/Vitest and current repository patterns. Mock the Effect/native settings interface, not raw Tauri internals.

App-shell/navigation tests:

- Settings exists on empty startup/no Current Workspace;
- Settings opens/closes through sidebar controls and current-page semantics switch correctly;
- same-Workspace view/search/selection/list-scroll/detail-scroll survive a Settings visit;
- hidden explorer is absent from accessibility queries;
- confirmed Workspace switch while Settings is open resets workspace-scoped context; pending/failure preserves it;
- settings value survives Workspace switch/removal/reset.

Settings model ordering tests with deferred promises:

- load missing -> default/no warning;
- load warning -> default/repair message;
- 18 save in flight, then 20, then 24 -> final backend call/confirmed value is 24 and 18 completion never marks Saved;
- older failure after newer intent does not replace draft/applied/retry target;
- newest failure keeps applied value and Retry saves it;
- navigating away during save still completes;
- valid edit after load warning clears warning only after success.

Input/page tests:

- valid boundaries 8 and 72, representative 24, arrow-step behavior;
- empty, fractional, 7, 73, and browser-normalized non-numeric/partial cases;
- invalid draft leaves preview and persistence untouched;
- Reset from valid, invalid, load-warning, and save-failed states;
- representative preview and shared renderer size;
- accessible name/description/error/status relationships;
- descriptions and comments receive the same applied value; app chrome does not.

## Likely Files

Adapt to merged code, likely including:

- `src/App.tsx` and `src/App.test.tsx`;
- a focused frontend settings state/Effect module under `src/settings/`;
- a Settings page module and focused tests under `src/settings/`;
- `src/issues/IssueExplorer.tsx` only for the validated font-size prop and deliberate preservation mechanics;
- existing typed RPC/Effect integration helpers.

Avoid placing form parsing, save queues, and error-state transitions directly as a large cluster of callbacks in `App`; keep `App` as composition/application of the deep settings module interface.

## Verification

- focused App Settings state/page/App tests;
- all frontend tests;
- TypeScript checking;
- Ultracite check/fix;
- React Doctor full regression scan;
- frontend build;
- relevant Rust/generated contract tests from dependency integration if types change during rebase.

## Out of Scope

- backend store/RPC implementation already owned by `bsm-al6.1`;
- Markdown ratio implementation already owned by `bsm-al6.2`;
- font-family controls or placeholders;
- separate code size;
- general routing/settings frameworks;
- per-Workspace preferences;
- cross-device sync/import/export;
- exhaustive desktop process-restart proof, owned by `bsm-al6.4`.

## Deviation Policy

Implementation details may adapt to the final Workspace shell, but do not sacrifice same-Workspace preservation or confirmed-switch reset semantics to simplify rendering. Stop and revise the bead before changing the valid range, replacing direct input with another control, adding an explicit Save button, reverting applied values on save failure, allowing concurrent stale writes, exposing hidden content to accessibility, or introducing a general router/state framework.
title: Add the Settings page and Markdown size input
